evals.query: Create a new Kora service in Java from scratch with Gradle.
expected_behavior: Creates build.gradle with the application and java plugins
Imports the BOM via koraBom platform("ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent:1.2.17") declared once
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
Defines an @KoraApp interface as the graph root
Does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora:* artifacts
Does not use foreign-framework annotations or field injection

evals.query: Set up the Gradle configurations so the Kora annotation processor resolves with the right version.
expected_behavior: Declares a custom koraBom configuration
Wires annotationProcessor.extendsFrom(koraBom) so the processor classpath gets the BOM
Extends implementation, compileOnly, and the test configurations from koraBom
Explains the annotation-processor classpath is separate from the application classpath
Does not put an explicit version on the annotation-processors dependency

evals.query: Write the main application class that boots the Kora graph.
expected_behavior: Annotates the interface with @KoraApp from ru.tinkoff.kora.common.KoraApp
Extends framework *Module interfaces such as UndertowHttpServerModule and HoconConfigModule
Calls KoraApplication.run(ApplicationGraph::graph) in main
Imports KoraApplication from ru.tinkoff.kora.application.graph
Does not reference a foreign-framework application-bootstrap annotation

evals.query: My Kora Java build produces no generated code and ApplicationGraph cannot be resolved.
expected_behavior: Identifies the missing annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" as the cause
Notes that ApplicationGraph is generated from the @KoraApp interface at compile time
Suggests running ./gradlew classes to trigger the processor
Points to build/generated/sources/annotationProcessor/ for the generated graph

evals.query: Which JDK and Gradle version should I use for a Kora Java project, and how do I configure the toolchain?
expected_behavior: States JDK 17 minimum, 21 recommended
Configures the Java toolchain with JavaLanguageVersion in build.gradle
Adds the foojay-resolver-convention plugin in settings.gradle
Notes Gradle 7+ is required and the wrapper pins a current distribution

evals.query: Add the Gradle setup so I can write a @KoraAppTest for my new Kora service.
expected_behavior: Adds testImplementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:test-junit5"
Adds testAnnotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors"
Enables useJUnitPlatform() in the test task
Uses @KoraAppTest with @TestComponent to inject graph components
Does not use a foreign-framework test annotation or a mock-bean construct

evals.query: I scaffolded a Kora app; how do I actually expose an HTTP endpoint?
expected_behavior: Adds implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:http-server-undertow"
Extends UndertowHttpServerModule in the @KoraApp interface
Creates a @Component @HttpController with an @HttpRoute method
Configures httpServer.publicApiHttpPort and privateApiHttpPort in application.conf
Uses constructor injection rather than field injection
